source: Phase 5 audit — every rule classified as core (canonical-state-only, fires on any OTel-instrumented stack) or augmentation (depends on specialization labels from a specific augmenter).

rules.name: RULE_CONTAINER_UNAVAILABLE_TO_SPAN
rules.description: Container UNAVAILABLE -> Pod -> Service -> Span MISSING/UNAVAILABLE

rules.name: RULE_CONTAINER_DEGRADED_TO_SPAN
rules.description: Container DEGRADED -> Pod -> Service -> Span SLOW/ERRORING

rules.name: RULE_CONTAINER_UNAVAILABLE_TO_POD
rules.description: Container UNAVAILABLE -> Pod DEGRADED (one bad container degrades the pod, structural).

rules.name: RULE_POD_UNAVAILABLE_TO_CONTAINER
rules.description: Pod UNAVAILABLE -> all Containers UNAVAILABLE (containment, structural).

rules.name: RULE_POD_UNAVAILABLE_TO_SERVICE
rules.description: Pod UNAVAILABLE -> Service UNAVAILABLE/DEGRADED (rollup; the only routable backend went away).

rules.name: RULE_POD_UNAVAILABLE_TO_SPAN
rules.description: Pod UNAVAILABLE -> Service -> Span MISSING/UNAVAILABLE (no requests served).

rules.name: RULE_POD_DEGRADED_TO_SPAN
rules.description: Pod DEGRADED -> Service -> Span SLOW/ERRORING (resource pressure causes latency).

rules.name: RULE_SERVICE_TO_SPAN
rules.description: Service-level fault propagates into its spans (first hop, lenient).

rules.name: RULE_SPAN_SLOW_TO_CALLER
rules.description: SLOW span propagates SLOW to its caller (callee->caller via reverse calls edge).

rules.name: RULE_SPAN_ERRORING_TO_CALLER
rules.description: ERRORING span propagates ERRORING to its caller.

rules.name: RULE_SPAN_UNAVAILABLE_TO_CALLER
rules.description: UNAVAILABLE/MISSING span propagates ERRORING/UNAVAILABLE to its caller.

tiers.core: Canonical-state predicates only; fire on any OTel-instrumented stack out-of-the-box. Returned by get_builtin_rules() by default.
tiers.augmentation: Depends on a specialization label that only specific augmenter adapters emit (e.g. frequent_gc, oom_killed, slow_db). Requires opt-in via get_builtin_rules(include_augmentation=True). Currently empty: rule_matcher.py predicates do not consume specialization labels, so any augmentation rule defined today would behave identically to its core counterpart. Augmentation rules will land alongside the JVM/OOM augmenter adapters in Phase 4 (#163).

design: All rules speak the canonical IR vocabulary: HEALTHY / SLOW / ERRORING / DEGRADED / RESTARTING / UNAVAILABLE / MISSING / UNKNOWN.

Specialization labels (e.g. high_cpu, crash_loop, pod_killed from K8sMetricsAdapter) ride on Evidence.specialization_labels and are surfaced via StateTimeline.labels_at — they do not constrain JSON-rule matching today.
Phase 2B-review cleanup: removed 'restarting' from RULE_POD_DEGRADED_TO_SPAN.src_states because no adapter emits pod.restarting (container restart -> container.unavailable + crash_loop instead).
